title: Field-Guide Outcomes, Command Language, and REPL Contract · Go Build Room
summary: A regional field-research team incrementally builds bounded field-guide product and REPL charter from original fixed PokéAPI fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP, Git, quality-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded field-guide product and REPL charter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Define learner, field user, decision, command sequence, accepted latency, failure consequence, and observable value before coding.
Specify command names, argument grammar, whitespace policy, help text, aliases, unknown-command behavior, and stable output contracts.
Model start, prompt, read, parse, dispatch, result, continue, EOF, quit, cancellation, and cleanup as explicit session states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go User Decision
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 5467 during a race-instrumented cache stress case. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate a useful, bounded, accessible REPL contract on observable command and lifecycle evidence; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then inject one redirect, control sequence, file, timeout, or persistence failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define learner, field user, decision, command sequence, accepted latency, failure consequence, and observable value before coding.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go User Decision.

For Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go User Decision, keep Pokedex Go User Decision connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 1515 during a clean cross-platform package smoke. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate a useful, bounded, accessible REPL contract on observable command and lifecycle evidence; failed and canceled commands must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one toolchain, target, artifact, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define runnable happy, changed, invalid, EOF, timeout, cancellation, and recovery cases plus exact stakeholder evidence.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Pokedex Go Acceptance Evidence happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Pokedex Go Acceptance Evidence condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
